etymology.description: Depicts a chin -> original form of 頤(頤) (yí, "chin")
etymology.interpretation: 𦣞 originally depicts the shape of a chin. This intuitive image underlies its proposed role as the early form of 頤(頤) (yí, "chin"), visually representing the anatomy it names. There is no continuation to mean 'many, numerous'; this seems a borrowing or error.
